anchored_threads.thread_name: The sthita-prajña / one whose insight is steady (verses 2.54-2.72)
anchored_threads.anchor_evidence: Indirect anchoring: 2.9's silence is the formal end of Arjuna's case before Krishna begins teaching. The chapter's pedagogical arc requires this scaffolding — without it the sthita-prajña teaching at the end of the chapter has no propaedeutic. The engineering layer correctly identifies 'the silence is structurally significant — it is the precondition for the senior teacher's reply in the next verse.' This anchors on the chapter's own pedagogy: scaffolding precedes the teaching of the steady-mind state.

progression_issue.explanation: Position 9 is the formal closure of Arjuna's case; the engineering layer treats it as such and does not preempt Krishna's reply at 2.11.

verdict_reason: BG 2.9 is the chapter's sanctioned scaffolding moment — the verse where Arjuna's case ends. The chapter's thesis explicitly treats narrative scaffolding as legitimate (it does not require every verse to contribute doctrine). The engineering layer correctly identifies the verse as scaffolding, refuses to manufacture doctrine, and explicitly disclaims the 'go silent before deciding' false-tactic read. The honest LOW + STRETCHED tagging is the chapter's sanctioned form for narrative verses. No hard edges violated, no counter-anti-thesis drift. PASS at the chapter's correct standard for narrative verses.
